books.title: Under the Hawthorn Tree
books.author: Marita Conlon-McKenna
books.rationale: This book is a fictional account of a family during the 1840s when Ireland was devastated by the potato famine. Although it is a dark time in history, this story also shows the resilience of the three siblings, who continue to care for one another despite hunger, fear, and constant uncertainty. This is an important event that helps students understand why so many Irish immigrants came to the US during this time period.

books.title: William Wilberforce: The Freedom Fighter
books.author: Derick Bingham
books.rationale: This biography tells about the man who dedicated his life to ending the slave trade and slavery in the British Empire. The book follows the life of William Wilberforce (1759-1833) beginning with his days as a school boy to being a member in Parliament who risked being thought a fool for standing by his principles. Students will not only learn about this man of God but also learn about what was happening in England during this time period.

books.title: Commodore Perry in the Land of the Shogun
books.author: Rhoda Blumberg
books.rationale: During this period in history, Japan had been isolated from the outside world for centuries. But when American Commodore Perry and his troops sailed to Japan with a letter from the President, everything began to change. This Newberry Honor book is an engaging way for students to explore global history, diplomacy, and cultural change through a well-told true story.
